senses.definition: the act of removing or erasing something, especially by scraping or rubbing it out
examples: The writer made several rubouts in the margin of the manuscript.
Rubouts of errors were done to correct the text.

senses.definition: a secret assassination; a killing carried out to remove someone from a position of power
examples: The dictator orchestrated several rubouts of political opponents.
Historically, political rubouts were common in the era.

senses.definition: a strip along the side of a ship or vehicle used to protect the edges from damage
examples: The rubrail protected the hull from minor collisions.
He leaned against the rubrail of the ferry.

senses.definition: an established set of guidelines or criteria for assessing or organizing something; a heading or category in a document
examples: The instructor used a rubric to grade essays.
The rubric for the report outlines several key sections.

senses.definition: a title or heading in a document or book that classifies content
examples: The rubric on the page indicated the chapter topic.
She followed the rubric to organize the notes.

senses.definition: a list or summary of items or categories; a brief catalog in some languages
examples: He consulted the rubrica of species in the atlas.
The ancient text contained a rubrica of commands.

senses.definition: relating to or contained in a rubric; categorized by rubric or headings
examples: The rubrical notes helped organize the procedure.
Rubrical guidelines were applied to the exam.

senses.definition: the quality of being rubrical; the use or presence of rubrics and headings
examples: The rubricality of the manuscript made its structure clear.
They judged with rubricality and clarity.

senses.definition: in a way that follows or uses a rubric or set of rules
examples: The essay was rubrically scored by the committee.
Activities were rubrically organized.

senses.definition: to apply or add a rubric or set of headings to something; to mark or annotate with a rubric
examples: He rubricated the form with clear section titles.
The document was rubricated to improve readability.

senses.definition: having been given a rubric or set of headings; annotated or labeled
examples: A rubricated form was handed to applicants.
The rubricated map shows the zones clearly.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of rubricate; applies a rubric or headings to something
examples: The editor rubricates pages for consistency.
She rubricates the forms before publishing.

senses.definition: the act of applying a rubric or headings to something
examples: Rubricating the document improved its structure.
Rubricating sections helps readers navigate.

senses.definition: the process of applying or creating rubrics; labeling or categorizing with headings
examples: Rubrication of the report made it easier to follow.
The rubrication of the forms was consistent.

senses.definition: instances or instances of rubrication
examples: Various rubrications were added to the document.
The rubrications improved clarity.

senses.definition: a person or device that applies or creates rubrics; a rubric-like instrument or tool
examples: The rubricator organized the notes by topic.
The computer program acted as a rubricator for the manuscript.

senses.definition: plural of rubricator; people or devices that apply rubrics
examples: Several rubricators were employed in the project.
The rubricators ensured consistency across documents.

senses.definition: a person who specializes in rubrics or organizes information by headings; a rubric expert
examples: The rubrician prepared a detailed grading scheme.
Rubricians study methods of categorization.

senses.definition: plural of rubrician; experts in rubrics or heading-based organization
examples: Rubricians gathered to discuss standardization.
The rubricians reviewed the categories.

senses.definition: excessive emphasis on rubrics or the use of rubrics; tendency to rely on guidelines
examples: The rubricism in grading limited creative expression.
Some critics call it rubricism in assessment.

senses.definition: a person who writes, designs, or critiques rubrics; an expert in rubric creation
examples: The rubricist drafted the scoring guide.
A rubricist advised on assessment standards.

senses.definition: the quality of being rubric-like; the state of being organized by rubrics
examples: Rubricity improved the readability of the manual.
The rubricity of the document helped navigation.

senses.definition: to apply a rubric or to categorize by headings; to label with a rubric
examples: They rubricize data for easier comparison.
The form was rubricized to standardize responses.

senses.definition: having a rubric-like or finely divided organization; marked by headings
examples: A rubricose outline made the plan clear.
The rubricose pages show clear sections.

senses.definition: plural of rubric; sets of guidelines or criteria for judging or categorizing
examples: The rubrics specify what counts as quality.
Different rubrics apply to different tasks.

senses.definition: making or capable of being rubricated; giving rise to headings or categories
examples: A rubrific document was easier to navigate.
Their rubrific approach helped organize data.

senses.definition: the process of adding rubrics or headings; the act of rubricating
examples: Rubrification helps standardize submissions.
The rubrification of the template was welcome.
